{"problem_standardized_math":"Prove formula (3.28) by carrying out the following steps. Using\n\\[\nx_{k+1}=x_k-\\left(\\frac{\\nabla f_k^T\\nabla f_k}{\\nabla f_k^TQ\\nabla f_k}\\right)\\nabla f_k,\n\\tag{3.26}\n\\]\nshow first that\n\\[\n\\|x_k-x^*\\|_Q^2-\\|x_{k+1}-x^*\\|_Q^2\n=2\\alpha_k\\nabla f_k^TQ(x_k-x^*)-\\alpha_k^2\\nabla f_k^TQ\\nabla f_k,\n\\]\nwhere \\(\\|\\cdot\\|_Q\\) is defined by\n\\[\n\\frac{1}{2}\\|x-x^*\\|_Q^2=f(x)-f(x^*).\n\\tag{3.27}\n\\]\nThen, using the fact that \\(\\nabla f_k=Q(x_k-x^*)\\), obtain\n\\[\n\\|x_k-x^*\\|_Q^2-\\|x_{k+1}-x^*\\|_Q^2\n=\n\\frac{2(\\nabla f_k^T\\nabla f_k)^2}{\\nabla f_k^TQ\\nabla f_k}\n-\n\\frac{(\\nabla f_k^T\\nabla f_k)^2}{\\nabla f_k^TQ\\nabla f_k},\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\|x_k-x^*\\|_Q^2=\\nabla f_k^TQ^{-1}\\nabla f_k.\n\\]\nHence prove that\n\\[\n\\|x_{k+1}-x^*\\|_Q^2\n=\n\\left\\{1-\n\\frac{(\\nabla f_k^T\\nabla f_k)^2}{(\\nabla f_k^TQ\\nabla f_k)(\\nabla f_k^TQ^{-1}\\nabla f_k)}\n\\right\\}\\|x_k-x^*\\|_Q^2.\n\\tag{3.28}\n\\]"}
